title: 10 medicinal constituents of Cypripedium × io (lady's slipper)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Cypripedium × io berberine is a unique plant species that exhibits remarkable properties. It is a hybrid of the Cypripedium genus and is characterized by its distinctive floral pattern resembling a slipper or a shoe. Berberine, a compound found in this plant, has been studied extensively for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties.

Cypripedium × io palmatine is a rare and exotic orchid hybrid. It is a cross between two species of Cypripedium, the yellow or pink-flowered lady's slipper orchids. Palmatine has showy, drooping flowers with five petals and sepals that resemble a slipper, typically blooming in shades of yellow or pink. This unique orchid hybrid is highly prized by collectors and enthusiasts due to its rarity and striking appearance.

Cypripedium × io epipalmitate is a unique and rare orchid hybrid. It is a cross between two species of lady's slippers, Cypripedium candidum and Cypripedium parviflorum, resulting in a distinct combination of characteristics. The plant has a yellow and brown coloration with a distinctive lip shape, resembling a slipper.

Cypripedium × io cypripedin is a rare and unusual orchid hybrid. It is a cross between two different species of lady's slipper orchids, Paphiopedilum and Cypripedium. The resulting plant has a unique appearance, with a slipper-shaped pouch in shades of pink and white, surrounded by green leaves.

Cypripedium × io glucosides is a type of bioactive compound found in various species of orchids. These glucosides have been traditionally used in traditional medicine for their potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Research has shown that they possess bioactivities such as inhibiting enzymes and exhibiting antibacterial activity, making them a subject of interest in the field of pharmaceuticals.

Cypripedium × io iodesmilin is a hybrid orchid species that belongs to the genus Cypripedium. This rare and endangered plant is a cross between Cypripedium parviflorum and Cypripedium reginae. It is characterized by its unique, slipper-shaped flowers with fringed petals and sepals in shades of yellow and green.

Cypripedium × io magnoflorine is a hybrid orchid species that combines the characteristics of two distinct parent species. It is a rare and unique plant found in North America, characterized by its showy, slipper-shaped flowers that range from yellow to orange in color. The magnoflorine content of this hybrid has been found to have potential medicinal properties, making it an interesting subject for botanical study and conservation efforts.

Cypripedium × io isoberperylene is a rare and endangered orchid species native to North America. It is a hybrid of the yellow lady's slipper (Cypripedium parviflorum) and the small white lady's slipper (Cypripedium candidum). This unique orchid has a distinctive pouch-like shape, with its petals fused together to form a slipper-like structure. Due to its rarity and limited habitat, conservation efforts are in place to protect this delicate species from extinction.

Cypripodium × io calceolarioside is a natural compound found in certain species of orchids. It has been discovered to possess various biological activities, including antibacterial and antifungal properties. The compound has been isolated from the orchid Cypripedium × io and has shown potential for use in medicine.

Cypripodium × io berbamine is a unique orchid hybrid that combines the characteristics of two parent species. This rare and endangered plant is found in the wild only in certain regions of North America and Asia. Berbamine is prized for its distinctive pouch-like shape, which resembles a slipper or a lady's shoe, hence its common name. Its flowers are highly sought after by collectors and enthusiasts due to their rarity and beauty.

intro: Cypripedium × io has active constituents such as alkaloids, saponins, and phenolic acids. These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues. For example, the alkaloids can help reduce pain and inflammation, while the antioxidants can protect against cell damage and improve overall well-being. By incorporating lady's slipper into their treatment regimens, patients may experience improved symptoms and enhanced quality of life.
intro_desc: Cypripedium × io has active constituents such as iridoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids. These constituents possess medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects. The iridoids, in particular, have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and lipid levels. Additionally, the flavonoids may enhance cognitive function and memory, while the alkaloids could exhibit antimicrobial and anticancer activities, potentially improving quality of life for individuals with chronic diseases.
